parameters.responseBody: ={{ { status: "ok", datasetId: $("Insert Record Manager").first().json.dataset_id, rowsInserted: $("Mapping Logic").first().json.total_row_count, datasetName:$("Mapping Logic").first().json.dataset_name } }}

parameters.text:
=Analyze the following dataset as a single block to build a SQL Search Index:
{{ JSON.stringify($input.all().map(i => i.json)) }}

options.systemMessage:
ROLE
You are a Senior Database Engineer and Metadata Architect. Your objective is to analyze a 300-row sample of CSV data and generate a highly structured metadata map. This map will be used by a downstream AI to construct 100% accurate, high-performance SQL queries.

YOUR TASK
Analyze the data and identify "Filtering Columns." These are columns used in SQL WHERE, HAVING, or GROUP BY clauses.

Categorical Discovery: Identify columns with low-to-medium cardinality (categories, statuses, regions, types). Extract the exact unique values.

Temporal & Numeric Discovery: Identify date/time columns and numerical columns. Note their exact formatting (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD, presence of $ or , in numbers) so the SQL agent knows how to cast or parse them.

Semantic Mapping: Infer the precise business logic of each column based on its header and values. Do not hallucinate; stick to what the data clearly represents.

Data Quality & Quirks: Detect if the column contains empty strings, NULL, N/A, or formatting quirks (e.g., case sensitivity, leading spaces).

Granularity/Double-Counting: Identify rows that represent Subtotals, Totals, or Rollups (e.g., a "Region" column that contains the value "All Regions").

STRICT OUTPUT FORMAT
You must output strictly valid JSON. Do not include markdown formatting, conversational filler, or explanations. Start immediately with the JSON array [.

Use this exact JSON schema:
[
  {
    "column_header": "Exact Column Name",
    "data_type": "Categorical | Date | Numeric | Boolean",
    "business_logic": "Brief, precise description of what this filters (e.g., Transaction lifecycle stage).",
    "exact_values": ["Value A", "Value B", "Value C"], 
    "format_notes": "For Dates: format like MM/DD/YYYY. For Numerics: note currency symbols or commas. For Categories: note case sensitivity or special characters.",
    "null_handling": "Note if empty strings, NULLs, or N/A are present.",
    "aggregation_warning": "Include specific instructions if this column contains 'Total', 'Subtotal', or 'All' values that must be excluded in standard SUM() queries to avoid double-counting. Otherwise, output null."
  }
]

CONSTRAINTS:

Ignore purely unique identifiers (e.g., Transaction ID, Employee SSN, full names) UNLESS they are highly relevant for grouping (e.g., Manager ID).

Limit exact_values to a maximum of 15 items. If there are more, list the top 10 and add "...[High Cardinality]".

Output ONLY the raw JSON array.

parameters.text:
=Description of the Dataset by User: {{ $('Webhook').first().json.body.dataset_desc }}

Dataset Summary: {{ $('Clean Summary').first().json.cleaned_summary }}

options.systemMessage:
You are a data analyst who analyzes datasets based on the Description of the Dataset by User and Dataset Summary to come up with a set of 3 questions or queries that would be made by a real user who would use the dataset to answer real world business questions.

Each question must consider:
- The nature of the dataset -- the subject matter of the data (e.g. sales funnel, revenue, list of employees, etc.), type of data (e.g. time-based? monthly / annual / time stamped log or book of records, etc.)
- Potential key takeaways (e.g. the number of deals in various deal stages?, total number of employees in different job level, total revenue, revenue variance year-over-year, total number of customers in the US, etc.)
- Other potential insights to the user that the dataset could bring

Each question must include:
- Structured terms and language that provides clarity for AI to build an effective and accurate SQL query

** IMPORTANT **
Do not put SQL query directly in the question. 
Be brief and talk in layman's terms that anyone can understand. (like to a 12-year old)
The response should be a list of 3 sample questions in JSON format.

parameters.text:
=Description of the Dataset by User: {{ $('Loop Over Items').item.json.dataset_desc }}

Dataset Summary: {{  $('Loop Over Items').item.json.dataset_summary }}

options.systemMessage:
You are a data analyst who analyzes datasets based on the Description of the Dataset by User and Dataset Summary to come up with a set of 5 questions or queries that would be made by a real user who would use the dataset to answer real world business questions.

Each question must consider:
- The nature of the dataset -- the subject matter of the data (e.g. sales funnel, revenue, list of employees, etc.), type of data (e.g. time-based? monthly / annual / time stamped log or book of records, etc.)
- Potential key takeaways (e.g. the number of deals in various deal stages?, total number of employees in different job level, total revenue, revenue variance year-over-year, total number of customers in the US, etc.)
- Other potential insights to the user that the dataset could bring

Each question must include:
- Structured terms and language that provides clarity for AI to build an effective and accurate SQL query

Do not put SQL query directly in the question. 
Be brief and talk in layman's terms that anyone can understand. (like to a 12-year old)
The response should be a list of 3 sample questions in JSON format.

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO n8n_data.dataset_record_manager (
    dataset_name,
    row_count,
    dataset_summary,
    column_mapping,
    column_dictionary,
    owner_email,
    dataset_desc
) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4::jsonb, $5::jsonb, $6, $7)
RETURNING dataset_id

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { status: "ok", datasetId: $("Insert Record Manager").first().json.dataset_id, rowsInserted: $("Mapping Logic").first().json.total_row_count, datasetName:$("Mapping Logic").first().json.dataset_name } }}
